full_response:
We start from the ordinary Schwarzschild line–element  

ds² = −(1 − 2GM/r) dt² + dr² /(1 − 2GM/r) + r² dΩ²,  dΩ² ≡ dθ² + sin²θ dφ².

The usual domain is r>0.  We are asked to look at a putative “fifth region’’ in which the radial coordinate takes negative values, r<0, and ask whether that side of the analytic extension becomes asymptotically flat when |r|→∞.

1.  Re-label the negative radius.  
   Introduce a positive variable  

  R ≡ −r   (R>0 when r<0).

   Because dr = −dR we have dr² = dR².  Substitute r = −R in the metric:

  1 − 2GM/r = 1 − 2GM/(−R) = 1 + 2GM/R.

   Therefore the metric in the r<0 region reads  

  ds² = −(1 + 2GM/R) dt² + dR² /(1 + 2GM/R) + R² dΩ².  (★)

2.  Behaviour for R→∞ (i.e. r→−∞).  
   Expand the bracket to first order in 1/R:

  (1 + 2GM/R) = 1 + O(1/R).

   Hence  

  g_tt = −(1 + 2GM/R) = −1 − 2GM/R + O(1/R²),  
  g_RR = 1 − 2GM/R + O(1/R²),  
  g_ΩΩ = R².

   As R→∞ the corrections O(1/R) vanish and we obtain  

  ds² → −dt² + dR² + R² dΩ²,

   the Minkowski metric in spherical coordinates.  The fall-off of g_ab − η_ab ∼ 1/R is exactly the behaviour required for an asymptotically flat space-time.

3.  Interpretation.  
   Comparing (★) with the far–field form of a Schwarzschild solution with mass parameter M̃,

  ds² ≃ −(1 − 2GM̃/R) dt² + (1 + 2GM̃/R) dR² + … ,

   we see that the coefficients match if M̃ = −M.  In other words, the r<0 sheet looks, from infinity, like a Schwarzschild space–time whose ADM mass is the negative of the original mass.  The fact that the mass comes out negative does not spoil asymptotic flatness; it only changes the sign of the 1/R corrections.

Therefore, although no causal curve can pass through r=0 to reach this sheet, the region with r<0 (or equivalently R>0) is indeed an asymptotically flat universe, but with a negative ADM mass.

\boxed{\text{Yes.  As }r\to -\infty\;(R\to\infty)\text{ the metric approaches Minkowski space; the region is asymptotically flat, appearing as a Schwarzschild space–time with mass }-M.}

Confidence: 78%
extracted_answer: \text{Yes.  As }r\to -\infty\;(R\to\infty)\text{ the metric approaches Minkowski space; the region is asymptotically flat, appearing as a Schwarzschild space–time with mass }-M.
